title: Multi-phase project resume and state validation
description: When resuming a multi-phase project, always validate actual system state before executing. Previous thread reported Phase 4 as 14% complete (8/57), but actual state was 100% (57/57) - Phases 4-5 were already complete. Validation prevented duplicate work.
context: User requested to 'boot up implementation and wrap it up' for CRM Unification. RESUME.md indicated Phase 4 incomplete, but actual index.jsonl showed full completion.
outcome: Validated state first: discovered Phases 4-5 were complete, correctly identified Phase 6-7 as next steps. Executed Phases 6-7 successfully without duplicating previous work.

title: Automated cleanup with dry-run validation
description: Phase 7 cleanup script demonstrated effective pattern: comprehensive dry-run preview showing all changes (21 files, 85 replacements, 13 archived files, 3 backup sets), user validation, then execution. Script included deprecation notices, path fixes, archival, and compression in single coordinated operation.
context: Post-unification cleanup required fixing legacy path references, archiving old directories, deprecating scripts, and compressing backups. Multiple subsystems affected, high risk of breaking references.
outcome: Zero errors. All legacy references fixed, N5/stakeholders/ properly archived, backups compressed 60%, deprecated scripts clearly marked. Verification confirmed 0 broken production references.

title: Phased cleanup operations: deprecate-archive-compress
description: Cleanup executed in three discrete phases: (1) Deprecate legacy scripts with clear notices, (2) Archive legacy directories to Documents/Archive/ with metadata, (3) Compress migration backups. Each phase validated independently before proceeding.
context: Legacy stakeholder system needed retirement after CRM unification. Active scripts still referenced old paths, directory contained operational files, multiple backup sets existed.
outcome: Clean separation of concerns. Legacy scripts remain functional but deprecated, archived content preserved with context, backups compressed from 341KB to 135KB (60% reduction). Each phase independently reversible.
